name: OAuth App Consent Abuse / Token Theft
description: Contain an adversary who obtained access via malicious OAuth app consent or stolen access/refresh tokens. Covers AiTM phishing and session hijacking patterns.

steps.name: Identify the malicious OAuth application
steps.description: Enumerate all OAuth apps with delegated permissions for the affected user. Flag any app not on the approved application inventory.
steps.dry_run_preview: Would query Microsoft Graph /me/oauth2PermissionGrants and compare against sanctioned app list.

steps.name: Revoke all refresh tokens (revokeSignInSessions)
steps.description: Immediately invalidate all issued tokens for the affected account. Access tokens (1hr TTL) remain valid until expiry — monitor during this window.
steps.dry_run_preview: Would POST to /users/{id}/revokeSignInSessions. All refresh tokens invalidated. Access tokens live up to 1 hour.

rollback.note: Token revocation is irreversible. User must re-authenticate.

steps.name: Remove the malicious OAuth app consent grant
steps.description: Delete the OAuth2PermissionGrant for the malicious application. This removes delegated access going forward.
steps.dry_run_preview: Would DELETE /oauth2PermissionGrants/{grant_id} for the identified malicious app.

rollback.note: User re-consents to legitimate apps if accidentally removed.

steps.name: Block the malicious application tenant-wide
steps.description: Disable the service principal for the malicious app in the tenant to prevent any other user from consenting to it.
steps.dry_run_preview: Would PATCH /servicePrincipals/{id} with accountEnabled: false. Blocks app for all users in tenant.

steps.name: Review and contain accessed data
steps.description: Determine what data the malicious app accessed via the delegated permissions. Check mail read, file access, and calendar events during the attack window.
steps.dry_run_preview: Would review audit logs for all API calls made by the malicious app's service principal during the compromise window.

steps.name: Enable conditional access policy — block legacy auth
steps.description: If legacy authentication protocols were exploited, enable a Conditional Access policy to block them tenant-wide.
steps.dry_run_preview: Would create CA policy: Block legacy authentication for all users. Test in report-only mode for 24h first.

evidence_checklist: OAuth app name, client ID, and permission scopes
Consent grant creation timestamp and actor
Token issuance logs during attack window
Data accessed via malicious app (mail, files, calendar)
